title: dysoxylum carolinae
intro: Dysoxylum carolinae, also known as the Carolina Dysoxylum or the White Dysoxylum, is a species of flowering plant in the family Meliaceae. It is a tree native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia and is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant's bark, leaves, and roots are used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, and have been shown to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory properties. Dysoxylum carolinae can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and respiratory infections.
